title: MERS-CoV Spike Glycoprotein ELISA Protocol
author: Mustapha Bittaye
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bgr6jv9e

steps.title: Print off a new ELISA record...
steps.instruction: Print off a new ELISA record sheet for each experiment (accessed from: X:\KEwer\7. ELISA\Templates and protocols).

steps.title: Number the experiment with the next...
steps.instruction: Number the experiment with the next experiment number and fill this in with the required information throughout the experiment.

steps.title: Calculate the number of Nunc Immuno...
steps.instruction: Calculate the number of Nunc Immuno ELISA plates required (One plate: 23 samples and an internal control in triplicate in addition to the standard curve in duplicate).

steps.title: Thaw an aliquot of MERS-CoV clamped spike GP (typically 0
steps.instruction: Thaw an aliquot of MERS-CoV clamped spike GP (typically 0.5 mg/ml stock solution kept at -80 °C and prepare as shown in the table below. Calculate accordingly if stock protein concentration differed). %s %s Number of plates to coat Volume of Spike GP (0.5 mg/ml stock solution) Volume of DPBS 1 10 µL 4.99 mL 2 20 µL 9.98 mL 3 30 µL 14.97 mL 4 40 µL 19.98 mL 5 50 µL 24.95 mL 6 60 µL 29.94 mL

steps.title: Coat plates with 50 µl/well of...
steps.instruction: Coat plates with 50 µl/well of MERS-CoV Spike clamped GP at 1.0 μg/ml in DPBS).

steps.title: Cover plates in clingfilm and store...
steps.instruction: Cover plates in clingfilm and store overnight for 18:00:00 at 4 °C . Note time of coating and record on the experiment record sheet.

steps.title: Thaw required serum samples (up to...
steps.instruction: Thaw required serum samples (up to 23 samples per plate) at Room temperature for at least 01:00:00 .

steps.title: Flick off coating solution into the sink
steps.instruction: Flick off coating solution into the sink. Wear eye protection.

steps.title: Wash plates 6x in DPBS/T using...
steps.instruction: Wash plates 6x in DPBS/T using the handheld plate washer and tap dry on paper towel between washes. After the final wash, invert plates and tap firmly. Leave plates on the bench for 2 min and tap firmly on a paper towel one more time to remove residual liquid from the wells

steps.title: Block plates with 100 µL, casein per well
steps.instruction: Block plates with 100 µL, casein per well .

steps.title: Stack plates (with an empty blank plate on top)
steps.instruction: Stack plates (with an empty blank plate on top). Leave for 01:00:00 at Room temperature . Note time of blocking and record on the experiment sheet.

steps.title: During blocking prepare serum samples and...
steps.instruction: During blocking prepare serum samples and reference standard dilutions as in section below.

steps.title: Vortex the thawed samples thoroughly and...
steps.instruction: Vortex the thawed samples thoroughly and dilute in casein at 1:500 ( 5 µL, sample diluted in 2495 µL, casein and vortexed). All sample dilutions MUST be done using a minimum of 5 µL, sample .

steps.title: Add 50 µL, diluted sample to...
steps.instruction: Add 50 µL, diluted sample to each well (each sample in triplicate). Store diluted serum Overnight in fridge in case repeat of samples at higher dilution is necessary.

steps.title: The standard curve is a two-fold...
steps.instruction: The standard curve is a two-fold dilution series with an initial dilution of 1:100 from the positive standard pool. To avoid multiple freeze-thaw of the positive standard pool, prepare aliquots of 1:100 working standards in casein to a final volume of 600 µL ( 6 µL, pool sample in 594 µL, casein ) and store in -80 °C freezer, where it is stable for up to a year .

steps.title: All standard dilution is done using...
steps.instruction: All standard dilution is done using Eppendorf tubes labelled Tube1 – Tube10. To run 2 plates prepare standards as follows: Add 520 µL of the 1:100 working standard to the first tube (Tube1). Add 260 µL, casein to the rest of the tubes (Tube2 – Tube10). Transfer 260 µL from Tube1 to Tube2 ( mix 15 times ). Transfer 260 µL from Tube2 into Tube3 and continue across the rest of the tubes. Discard 260 µL from the last tube (Tube10).

steps.title: Make a 1:800 dilution of the...
steps.instruction: Make a 1:800 dilution of the standard serum pool in casein to use for the plate internal control. (eg 175 µL, casein + 25 µL, positive standard pool per plate). Add to wells D12, E12 and F12.

steps.title: If one plate is assayed, refreeze...
steps.instruction: If one plate is assayed, refreeze the remaining 1:100 working standard serum and reuse in subsequent assays. This can be freeze-thawed up to three times if required.

steps.title: After 1 h incubation for blocking,...
steps.instruction: After 1 h incubation for blocking, flick off blocking solution into the sink and tap dry.

steps.title: Add 50 µL of the thoroughly...
steps.instruction: Add 50 µL of the thoroughly vortexed diluted samples (in triplicate) and the blank control (casein only) as shown in the plate layout below using an automatic pipette if available. Without adding the standards and internal control , cover plate with an empty blank plate and repeat step if multiple plates are prepared. %s %s Sample 1 Sample 2 Sample 3 Sample 4 Sample 5 Sample 6 Sample 7 Sample 8 Sample 9 Sample 10 Sample 11 Sample 12 Sample 1 Sample 2 Sample 3 Sample 4 Sample 5 Sample 6 Sample 7 Sample 8 Sample 9 Sample 10 Sample 11 Sample 12 Sample 1 Sample 2 Sample 3 Sample 4 Sample 5 Sample 6 Sample 7 Sample 8 Sample 9 Sample 10 Sample 11 Sample 12 Sample 13 Sample 14 Sample 15 Sample 16 Sample 17 Sample 18 Sample 19 Sample 20 Sample 21 Sample 22 Sample 23 Internal control Sample 13 Sample 14 Sample 15 Sample 16 Sample 17 Sample 18 Sample 19 Sample 20 Sample 21 Sample 22 Sample 23 Internal control Sample 13 Sample 14 Sample 15 Sample 16 Sample 17 Sample 18 Sample 19 Sample 20 Sample 21 Sample 22 Sample 23 Internal control St 1:100 St 1:200 St 1:400 St 1:800 St 1:1600 St 1:3200 St 1:6400 St 1:12800 St 1:25600 St 1:51200 Blank Blank St 1:100 St 1:200 St 1:400 St 1:800 St 1:1600 St 1:3200 St 1:6400 St 1:12800 St 1:25600 St 1:51200 Blank Blank

steps.title: Add 50 µL of the 1:100...
steps.instruction: Add 50 µL of the 1:100 Standard as shown in the plate layout to all plates first then followed by 1:200 and so on. Add the internal control immediately after adding the 1:800 standard then followed by the rest of the standards. Use an automatic pipette if available for transferring the standards. %s %s Sample 1 Sample 2 Sample 3 Sample 4 Sample 5 Sample 6 Sample 7 Sample 8 Sample 9 Sample 10 Sample 11 Sample 12 Sample 1 Sample 2 Sample 3 Sample 4 Sample 5 Sample 6 Sample 7 Sample 8 Sample 9 Sample 10 Sample 11 Sample 12 Sample 1 Sample 2 Sample 3 Sample 4 Sample 5 Sample 6 Sample 7 Sample 8 Sample 9 Sample 10 Sample 11 Sample 12 Sample 13 Sample 14 Sample 15 Sample 16 Sample 17 Sample 18 Sample 19 Sample 20 Sample 21 Sample 22 Sample 23 Internal control Sample 13 Sample 14 Sample 15 Sample 16 Sample 17 Sample 18 Sample 19 Sample 20 Sample 21 Sample 22 Sample 23 Internal control Sample 13 Sample 14 Sample 15 Sample 16 Sample 17 Sample 18 Sample 19 Sample 20 Sample 21 Sample 22 Sample 23 Internal control St 1:100 St 1:200 St 1:400 St 1:800 St 1:1600 St 1:3200 St 1:6400 St 1:12800 St 1:25600 St 1:51200 Blank Blank St 1:100 St 1:200 St 1:400 St 1:800 St 1:1600 St 1:3200 St 1:6400 St 1:12800 St 1:25600 St 1:51200 Blank Blank

steps.title: Incubate plate at Room temperature for...
steps.instruction: Incubate plate at Room temperature for 02:00:00 with a blank plate on top.

steps.title: After 2 h incubation with samples,...
steps.instruction: After 2 h incubation with samples, wash plates 6x in DPBS/T using the handheld plate washer as previously described.

steps.title: Dilute anti-human IgG antibody alkaline phosphatase...
steps.instruction: Dilute anti-human IgG antibody alkaline phosphatase 1:1000 in casein (see section ' Materials ').

steps.title: Add 50 µL, detection antibody to each well
steps.instruction: Add 50 µL, detection antibody to each well.

steps.title: Incubate plate at Room temperature for...
steps.instruction: Incubate plate at Room temperature for 01:00:00 with a blank plate on top.

steps.title: Prepare the pNPP substrate (development buffer)...
steps.instruction: Prepare the pNPP substrate (development buffer) as shown in the table below. Make up the substrate during the antibody incubation, at least 00:30:00 prior to development to allow adequate time for the pNPP tablet to dissolve and protect from light until use (see section ' Materials '). %s %s Number of plates to develop Number of pNPP tablets required Volume of 5X DEA buffer required Volume of H20 1 1 4 mL 16 mL 2 1 4 mL 16 mL 3 2 8 mL 32 mL 4 2 8 mL 32 mL 5 3 12 mL 48 mL 6 3 12 mL 48 mL

steps.title: After 1 h incubation with secondary...
steps.instruction: After 1 h incubation with secondary antibody, wash plates 6x in DPBS/T using the handheld plate washer as previously described.

steps.title: Add 100 µL, development buffer to each well
steps.instruction: Add 100 µL, development buffer to each well. Add the development buffer to the internal control column (Column 12) immediately after the Column containing standard 4 (Column 4) and then continue from Columns 5 – 11.

steps.title: Turn the plate reader on and...
steps.instruction: Turn the plate reader on and log in to the computer.

steps.title: Open the Gen5 software, create new...
steps.instruction: Open the Gen5 software, create new experiment using the protocol template (MERS STANDARDISED ELISA_SAMPLE ANALYSIS TEMPLATE) located in this folder (X:\Vaccine\10. MERS-CoV\1. MERS001_Oxford\12.0 Immunology\8. ELISA\Data and analysis\MERS001 STANDARDISED ELISA_SAMPLE ANALYSIS DATA).

steps.title: Start taking readings of the plate...
steps.instruction: Start taking readings of the plate at OD405 after 00:10:00 and repeat readings until the control readings and curve parameters fit expected values. internal control OD (wells D12, E12, F12) should be around 0.9-1.1 (1300-2300 EU) St 1:100 wells (G1 and H1) should be 3.5-3.8 St 1:800 wells (G4 and H4) should be close to the values in the internal control wells (D12, E12, F12). Blank wells should be <0.2 Standard curve reaches 4 parameter ranges below: %s %s Parameters A B C D R2 Ideal range 0.1-0.2 1-1.3 5-13 4.5-6 >0.996

steps.title: Export the plate data, standard curve...
steps.instruction: Export the plate data, standard curve and curve results to Excel. Record time of reads. Save the Gen5 Experiment and the Excel worksheet in the corresponding experiment folder. Trial specific assay data must be stored in the corresponding clinical trial folder on the X drive or for blinded studies use X:\KEwer\7.ELISA\ 14.Confidential Immunology Data.

steps.title: Analyse the data in the Excel...
steps.instruction: Analyse the data in the Excel sheet “ELISA analysis template” (accessed from: X:\KEwer\7. ELISA\Templates and protocols). All blue-shaded fields in the worksheets MUST be completed or confirmed as correct.

steps.title: Fill in the assay information and...
steps.instruction: Fill in the assay information and plate layout in the blue boxes in the “Plate layouts and raw data” page, then paste the plate layout, plate data and parameters (A,B,C,D and R 2 ) into the “plate layout and data” sheet.

steps.title: Check for outliers (“high CV” on...
steps.instruction: Check for outliers (“high CV” on Results page) and take average of duplicates instead of triplicates if necessary. On the Raw data, highlight outlying well in yellow, calculate the average of the other two wells and type this into the yellow outlier cell.

steps.title: The ELISA units for each sample...
steps.instruction: The ELISA units for each sample are on the Results sheet.

steps.title: Individual plate data and QC can...
steps.instruction: Individual plate data and QC can be seen on the individual plate sheets.

steps.title: The sheet will indicate if a...
steps.instruction: The sheet will indicate if a sample dilution is too high or low. If the sheet indicates that sample dilution is “TOO LOW” at 1:100, the sample is negative and can be assigned an arbitrary value of 1.

steps.title: If the sheet indicates that sample...
steps.instruction: If the sheet indicates that sample dilution is too low at 1:500 repeat at 1:100. For samples that are too high at 1:500 repeat them at higher dilution e.g. 1:1000, 1:2000 or more. When analysing samples that have been retested at 1:100 or higher dilutions such as 1:1000, change the dilution in the blue boxes on the individual plate pages (“plate 1”, “plate 2”, “plate 3” etc.)

steps.title: If further dilutions from the 1:500...
steps.instruction: If further dilutions from the 1:500 dilutions are not required then the tubes can be discarded.

steps.title: Mark samples with values greater than...
steps.instruction: Mark samples with values greater than 225 ELISA units as positive.

steps.title: Required
steps.instruction: Required: Add all assay parameter data for each experiment to a designated QC workbook.

steps.title: Required
steps.instruction: Required: QC all ELISA data for clinical trials prior to publication.

steps.title: Wipe dry the bottom of the...
steps.instruction: Wipe dry the bottom of the plate with clean tissue paper and place the plate in the plate reader.
